clean_story:
Cosmic dust clouds. Gravity sculpts them.
The name galaxy comes from the Greek gala, meaning milk, like the Milky Way’s pale band across the night sky.
A cloud of gas and dust drifts in space, nearly invisible. Gravity pulls particles together, like iron filings to a magnet. The cloud collapses, spinning faster as it shrinks, flattening into a disk. Stars ignite within it, their light revealing the new galaxy. The Andromeda Galaxy formed this way, its 1 trillion stars born from such a collapse.
Three related subjects are dark matter, star clusters and cosmic inflation.
